key_insight: Low-load CeO2–MnOx/C catalysts tune surface chemistry to favor the 2e- oxygen reduction pathway, enabling efficient H2O2 electrogeneration with up to ~90% selectivity.
engineering_relevance: The study points to a cost-effective, non-noble-metal catalyst design strategy for green hydrogen peroxide production using minimal metal loading on carbon supports.
method: Synthesized CeO2 and CeO2MnOx nanoparticles and supported them on Vulcan XC-72 carbon at 1, 3, and 5 wt%.
Characterized structure and surface properties with XRD, TEM, XPS, EPR, and contact-angle measurements.
Evaluated ORR performance and H2O2 selectivity using RRDE electrochemistry.
evidence_signal: RRDE data showed improved ORR activity and high H2O2 selectivity, with 1% CeO2MnOx/C and 3% CeO2/C giving the strongest performance and elevated ring currents.
watch_item: Performance appears sensitive to metal loading and surface modification, so scaling will need careful control of dispersion, hydrophilicity, and catalyst stability.
paper_title: Influence of CeO$_2$MnO$_x$ heterostructure on Hydrogen Peroxide Electrogeneration on Carbon-Based Catalysts
